%% -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
%% ROLE: engine-vendored — DO NOT edit here. `scitex-writer update-project`
%% overwrites this file on every re-vendor; fix it upstream in the
%% scitex-writer package instead (local edits are lost, and update-project
%% may set it read-only in the consumer workspace after vendoring).
%% File: 00_shared/latex_styles/clew_presentation.tex
%% Purpose: STATIC style for the clew presentation layer -- the RENDERING half.
%%
%%   SCOPE BOUNDARY (operator-ratified): scitex-writer owns the RENDERING (this
%%   file: the LaTeX macros, ulem wavy-underline composition, badge/colophon/
%%   legend layout, hyperlinks, icon). scitex-clew owns WHAT to render (the data:
%%   which claims, their values, the verdict->color mapping, the palette,
%%   badge states). So writer does NOT parse claims.json or decide colors --
%%   it consumes a clew-emitted include.
%%
%%   CONTRACT -- scitex-clew emits 00_shared/clew_rendered.tex defining (all
%%   inside \makeatletter ... \makeatother, claim_id sanitized to [a-zA-Z0-9]):
%%     \definecolor{clewVerified}{HTML}{..} (+ clewSuspect/clewUnverified/
%%       clewException) -- the 4-state palette (clew's; writer ships
%%       \providecolor fallbacks). Every status, incl. exception, is a COLOR
%%       (no badge glyph). 4 states: verified / suspect / unverified / exception.
%%     \@namedef{clew@val@<id>}{<value>}    -- registered claim_value (escaped).
%%     \@namedef{clew@hex@<id>}{<6hex>}     -- final per-claim color.
%%     \@namedef{clew@status@<id>}{<status>}.
%%     \def\clew@total{N} \def\clew@verified{M} \def\clew@allverified{0|1}.
%%   The OPT-IN toggles (markers/badge/signature/legend) are WRITER presentation
%%   config -- set by the manuscript preamble or a writer-side config step, NOT
%%   by clew. (demo/clew_rendered.sample.tex is a hand-written stand-in for
%%   clew's output, for host-compiling this renderer.)
%%
%%   Renders (each an INDEPENDENT opt-in toggle, all default OFF):
%%     markers   : \clewval{id} / \clewmark{id}{text} -> a status-colored
%%                 wavy underline (\uwave); 4 states incl. exception.
%%     badge     : a "Clew Verified" stamp, hyperlinked to the scitex-clew repo.
%%     signature : a subtle "Compiled by SciTeX Writer." colophon with the navy
%%                 snake icon, hyperlinked to the scitex-writer repo.
%%     legend    : a small key of the status colors.
%%
%%   SSoT vs decoration: \clewval{id} ALWAYS substitutes the registered value
%%   (data layer); the wave+color+badges are the opt-in `markers` decoration.
%%   So markers render INDEPENDENT of the #191 verify gate (an unverified value
%%   shows RED here even when the gate would block).
%%
%%   A simultaneous highlight was intentionally dropped: soul \hl cannot coexist
%%   with ulem \uwave (nesting swallows the argument) nor with the inline math in
%%   real claim values. The verdict-colored wavy underline is the robust spec.

%% --- packages (xcolor/hyperref/graphicx are loaded by packages.tex) ---
\RequirePackage[normalem]{ulem}   % \uwave; normalem keeps \emph italic
\IfFileExists{fontawesome5.sty}{\RequirePackage{fontawesome5}}{%
  \providecommand{\faCheckCircle}{[ok]}%
  \providecommand{\faExclamationTriangle}{[!]}}
%% \pdftooltip (pdfcomment) renders the per-claim provenance tooltip on each
%% marker. packages.tex loads pdfcomment BEFORE this style, so the real macro
%% wins; this \providecommand is a FALLBACK so a standalone / pdfcomment-less
%% compile degrades to the plain visible content instead of an undefined-macro
%% error (tooltip silently dropped, marker still renders).
\providecommand{\pdftooltip}[2]{#1}

%% --- opt-in toggles (default false; clew_rendered.tex flips per config) ---
\newif\ifclewpresmarkers
\newif\ifclewpresbadge
\newif\ifclewpressignature
\newif\ifclewpreslegend
\newif\ifclewpresattest      % "Provenance checked by SciTeX Clew." line
\newif\ifclewpresexplainer   % renderable clew explainer section
\newif\ifclewpresintro       % top-of-document "Provenance marks" intro section
                             % (auto-injected after \end{frontmatter} by the
                             % compile pipeline). Replaces the old bottom legend.
\newif\ifclewpreslegendfirst % opt-in: emit \clewLegend at the TOP of page 1
                             % (config key legend_first). Independent of
                             % \ifclewpreslegend (end-of-doc) and NOT in the
                             % master set, so master-on won't double-render it.

%% --- verdict palette ---
%% scitex-clew OWNS the palette (what to render). It emits \definecolor{clewX}
%% in clew_rendered.tex. These \providecolor lines are only a writer-side
%% FALLBACK so the doc still compiles if clew did not emit a palette.
%% CUD-safe fallback palette -- clew's canonical colorblind-safe reader hexes
%% (match clew's emitted display_palette; all clear the dE>=12 CVD floor). clew's
%% emitted \definecolor overrides these. Operator (Q1=b): provenance marks use
%% clew's CUD-safe palette, not the raw figrecipe hexes (green/yellow collapse
%% under protanopia). figrecipe colors are luminance-tuned, not CVD-categorical.
\providecolor{clewVerified}{HTML}{2DA44E}
\providecolor{clewSuspect}{HTML}{D29922}
\providecolor{clewUnverified}{HTML}{CF222E}
%% exception is conveyed by its own wavy-underline COLOR (no badge glyph).
%% clew provides the real color (overrides this placeholder).
\providecolor{clewException}{HTML}{8250DF}

%% --- signature config (overridable before \begin{document}) ---
%% No trailing period so the tool version reads cleanly after it ("... SciTeX
%% Writer v2.26.0"), symmetric with \clewAttestText. The " vX.Y.Z" suffix is
%% appended by \writerVersionSuffix (see \clewSignature below), mirroring how
%% \clewVersionSuffix appends the clew version to the attestation line.
\providecommand{\clewSigText}{Compiled by SciTeX Writer}
\providecommand{\clewSigURL}{https://github.com/ywatanabe1989/scitex-writer}
\providecommand{\clewBadgeURL}{https://github.com/ywatanabe1989/scitex-clew}
\providecommand{\clewSigIcon}{docs/scitex-icon-navy-inverted.png}
%% Clew attestation line (clew owns the text; writer renders it). Default
%% overridable by clew_rendered.tex / the manuscript. "audited" (honest) not
%% "checked/verified"; no trailing period so the tool version reads cleanly
%% after it ("... SciTeX Clew v0.8.0").
\providecommand{\clewAttestText}{Provenance audited by SciTeX Clew}
\providecommand{\clewAttestURL}{https://github.com/ywatanabe1989/scitex-clew}
%% Clew explainer body (clew provides the CONTENT; writer provides the section
%% layout). FAIL-LOUD default (operator incident 2026-07-07): a writer-invented
%% prose default here once rendered into the PDF unannounced -- a No-Silent-
%% Fallback violation (and its wording had Clew as the grammatical subject
%% when the manuscript should be, per operator feedback). Do NOT invent
%% replacement prose; when clew hasn't supplied its own \clewExplainerBody
%% (via clew_rendered.tex), show a visible pending-marker + compile warning
%% instead of guessing text on clew's behalf.
\providecommand{\clewExplainerBody}{%
  \PackageWarningNoLine{scitex-writer}{%
    Clew provenance wording (\string\clewExplainerBody) not supplied --
    rendering a pending-marker instead of inventing prose on clew's behalf.
    Provide it via clew_rendered.tex (scitex-clew's export step) to show the
    real explanation}%
  \textit{Provenance wording pending from SciTeX Clew.}}

%% Marker composition: highlight (tint of verdict) + wavy underline (verdict).
%% ALL-LETTER names (no @): these are called from the expl3 \clewval/\clewmark
%% below, and under \ExplSyntaxOn '@' is catcode-12 "other" -- a \clew@... name
%% would split at \clew and print the "@decorate..." tail as raw text.
%% #1 = content. Renders a verdict-COLORED wavy underline (ulem \uwave) -- the
%% original render spec ("wavy underline in clew node colors"). The verdict
%% color `clewVerdict` is \definecolor'd by the (expl3) caller before this runs.
%%
%% NOTE: a simultaneous soul \hl HIGHLIGHT was dropped. soul cannot coexist
%% with (a) ulem \uwave nested in \hl (swallows the argument; \SOUL@n errors)
%% nor (b) the inline math ($\times$, $>$, $|\delta|<$) present in real claim
%% values. ulem \uwave handles both robustly. A highlight-too needs a non-soul
%% mechanism (tikz background / lua-ul under xelatex) -- deferred to the operator.
\newcommand{\clewDecorate}[1]{%
  {\color{clewVerdict}\uwave{#1}}}

%% Figure provenance CHIP: a small verdict-colored filled square, appended to a
%% figure caption (a wavy underline is for inline text/cites; a figure mark is a
%% discrete chip). Uses clewVerdict, \definecolor'd by \clewfig before this runs.
\newcommand{\clewFigChip}{\,{\color{clewVerdict}\rule[0pt]{0.6em}{0.6em}}}

%% --- \clewval / \clewmark (expl3: robust id-sanitize + csname lookup) ---
%% claim_id is sanitized to [a-zA-Z0-9] -- the SAME transform scitex-clew
%% applies when emitting the macro names -- so authors write the raw claim_id.
\ExplSyntaxOn
\tl_new:N \l__clew_key_tl
\tl_new:N \l__clew_hex_tl
\tl_new:N \l__clew_val_tl
\tl_new:N \l__clew_tip_tl
\cs_new_protected:Npn \__clew_key:n #1 {
  \str_set:Nn \l_tmpa_str {#1}
  \regex_replace_all:nnN { [^a-zA-Z0-9] } {} \l_tmpa_str
  \tl_set:Nx \l__clew_key_tl { \str_use:N \l_tmpa_str }
}
%% \__clew_tip:nn {sanitized-key} {visible-content}: wrap the visible marker
%% content in a \pdftooltip whose text is the claim's provenance -- its status
%% and, when a source pointer was emitted, "status --- source: <link>". GRACEFUL
%% degradation (mirrors the existing \cs_if_exist fallbacks): when clew@status@
%% <key> is undefined (clew off / no data) the content renders with NO tooltip;
%% when status IS defined but clew@link@<key> is not (claim has no link) the
%% tooltip shows status only. #1 is passed the key tl, expanded inside the :c/:x
%% csname lookups -- the same \use:c mechanism as \clewval/\clewmark.
\cs_new_protected:Npn \__clew_tip:nn #1#2 {
  \cs_if_exist:cTF { clew@status@ #1 } {
    \tl_set:Nv \l__clew_tip_tl { clew@status@ #1 }
    \cs_if_exist:cT { clew@link@ #1 } {
      \tl_put_right:Nn \l__clew_tip_tl { ~---~source:~ }
      %% :Nv (value-expansion, NOT :Nx) keeps the link's \_ / \# / \% escape
      %% tokens INTACT so hyperref's \pdfstringdef converts them to the literal
      %% char; x-expanding them here shatters \_ into low-level tokens ->
      %% "Token not allowed in a PDF string" -> the char is dropped from the tip.
      \tl_put_right:Nv \l__clew_tip_tl { clew@link@ #1 }
    }
    \pdftooltip {#2} { \tl_use:N \l__clew_tip_tl }
  } {
    #2
  }
}
%% \clewval{id}: substitute the registered value (always); decorate if markers on.
%% \legacy_if:nTF tests the LaTeX2e \ifclewpresmarkers newif from expl3.
\NewDocumentCommand \clewval { m } {
  \__clew_key:n {#1}
  \cs_if_exist:cTF { clew@val@ \l__clew_key_tl } {
    \legacy_if:nTF { clewpresmarkers } {
      \tl_set:Nx \l__clew_hex_tl { \use:c { clew@hex@ \l__clew_key_tl } }
      \definecolor { clewVerdict } { HTML } { \l__clew_hex_tl }
      %% One-level expand the value to LITERAL tokens (spaces, $...$, \_ kept)
      %% then pass them via :NV so \uwave sees real space tokens and can break
      %% mid-value. Passing the bare \use:c macro gave \uwave one unbreakable
      %% chunk -> right-margin overflow (whereas \clewmark's literal #2 wraps).
      \tl_set:Nv \l__clew_val_tl { clew@val@ \l__clew_key_tl }
      \__clew_tip:nn { \l__clew_key_tl }
        { \exp_args:NV \clewDecorate \l__clew_val_tl }
    } {
      \use:c { clew@val@ \l__clew_key_tl }
    }
  } {
    \msg_warning:nnn { clew } { unknown-claim } {#1}
    \texttt { [clew: \tl_to_str:n {#1} ?] }
  }
}
%% \clewmark{id}{text}: mark author-supplied text/prose (decoration only).
\NewDocumentCommand \clewmark { m m } {
  \__clew_key:n {#1}
  \legacy_if:nTF { clewpresmarkers } {
    \cs_if_exist:cTF { clew@hex@ \l__clew_key_tl } {
      \tl_set:Nx \l__clew_hex_tl { \use:c { clew@hex@ \l__clew_key_tl } }
      \definecolor { clewVerdict } { HTML } { \l__clew_hex_tl }
      \__clew_tip:nn { \l__clew_key_tl } { \clewDecorate {#2} }
    } {
      #2
    }
  } {
    #2
  }
}
%% \clewcite{bibkey}: \cite the key with a verdict-colored wavy underline
%% (parallel to \clewval -- same 4-state color). Falls back to a plain \cite
%% when markers are off OR the key is not a registered citation-claim, so it is
%% always safe to use in place of \cite. Matches by claim_id == the BibTeX key
%% (sanitized to [a-zA-Z0-9], same as clew emits).
\NewDocumentCommand \clewcite { m } {
  \__clew_key:n {#1}
  \legacy_if:nTF { clewpresmarkers } {
    \cs_if_exist:cTF { clew@hex@ \l__clew_key_tl } {
      \tl_set:Nx \l__clew_hex_tl { \use:c { clew@hex@ \l__clew_key_tl } }
      \definecolor { clewVerdict } { HTML } { \l__clew_hex_tl }
      \__clew_tip:nn { \l__clew_key_tl } { \clewDecorate { \cite {#1} } }
    } {
      \cite {#1}
    }
  } {
    \cite {#1}
  }
}
%% \clewfig{figure_id}: a verdict-colored provenance chip for a figure caption
%% (figure_id = the figure save-path == the join key). Renders NOTHING when
%% markers are off or the figure is not a registered figure-claim, so it is safe
%% wherever figrecipe emits it into a caption.
\NewDocumentCommand \clewfig { m } {
  \__clew_key:n {#1}
  \legacy_if:nTF { clewpresmarkers } {
    \cs_if_exist:cTF { clew@hex@ \l__clew_key_tl } {
      \tl_set:Nx \l__clew_hex_tl { \use:c { clew@hex@ \l__clew_key_tl } }
      \definecolor { clewVerdict } { HTML } { \l__clew_hex_tl }
      \__clew_tip:nn { \l__clew_key_tl } { \clewFigChip }
    } { }
  } { }
}
\msg_new:nnn { clew } { unknown-claim }
  { Clew~claim~'#1'~not~found~in~clew_rendered.tex~(rendering~plain). }
\ExplSyntaxOff

\makeatletter

%% --- "Clew Verified" badge (hyperlinked to the clew repo) ---
\newcommand{\clewVerifiedBadge}{%
  \ifnum\clew@allverified=1
    \href{\clewBadgeURL}{\colorbox{clewVerified!12!white}{%
      \textcolor{clewVerified}{\small\faCheckCircle\ Clew Verified}}}%
  \else
    \href{\clewBadgeURL}{\colorbox{clewUnverified!12!white}{%
      \textcolor{clewUnverified}{\small\faExclamationTriangle\ %
        \clew@total\ claims, \the\numexpr\clew@total-\clew@verified\relax\ unverified}}}%
  \fi}

%% --- bottom-RIGHT colophon + attestation (both carry the snake icon) ---
%% The snake icon (writer-repo for the colophon, clew-repo for the attestation
%% per the operator -- same snake on both lines).
%% Force-expand \clewSigIcon to a LITERAL path before \IfFileExists so the
%% guard actually tests the file. Passing the bare macro (\IfFileExists{\clewSigIcon})
%% can leave it unexpanded (texlive vintage / flatten context), so the false
%% branch never fires and a MISSING icon crashes \includegraphics with
%% "File `\clewSigIcon ' not found". With the icon absent this now degrades to a
%% text-only colophon instead of a fatal error.
\newcommand{\clewColophonIcon}{%
  \begingroup\edef\clew@sigiconpath{\clewSigIcon}%
  \expandafter\IfFileExists\expandafter{\clew@sigiconpath}%
    {\raisebox{-0.25em}{\includegraphics[height=1em]{\clew@sigiconpath}}\,}{}%
  \endgroup}
%% "Compiled by SciTeX Writer vX.Y.Z" -> writer repo, right-aligned. The version
%% suffix MIRRORS the clew attestation line: \writerVersionSuffix appends
%% " vX.Y.Z" from \writer@version (stamped by the compile pipeline at compile
%% time), or nothing when unavailable (graceful fallback to a version-less line).
\newcommand{\clewSignature}{%
  \par\vspace{0.8em}%
  {\footnotesize\color{gray}\raggedleft
    \href{\clewSigURL}{\clewColophonIcon\clewSigText\writerVersionSuffix}\par}}
%% Tool-version suffix: " vX.Y.Z" when render_clew stamped \clew@version (from
%% the clew export or `clew --version`); nothing when absent. \@empty test =
%% the \providecommand{}{} fallback below.
\newcommand{\clewVersionSuffix}{%
  \ifx\clew@version\@empty\else\ v\clew@version\fi}
%% Writer tool-version suffix: the WRITER analog of \clewVersionSuffix. " vX.Y.Z"
%% when the compile stamped \writer@version (compile_tex_structure.py injects the
%% LIVE scitex-writer version via resolve_writer_version()); nothing when absent,
%% so a standalone/pipeline-less compile degrades to "Compiled by SciTeX Writer"
%% and NEVER breaks. Clew stamps the version that AUDITED the doc; writer stamps
%% the version that COMPILED it -- the freshest source, not the stale vendored
%% \ScitexWriterVersion.
\newcommand{\writerVersionSuffix}{%
  \ifx\writer@version\@empty\else\ v\writer@version\fi}
%% "Provenance audited by SciTeX Clew vX.Y.Z" -> clew repo, right-aligned, same snake.
\newcommand{\clewAttestation}{%
  {\footnotesize\color{gray}\raggedleft
    \href{\clewAttestURL}{\clewColophonIcon\clewAttestText\clewVersionSuffix}\par}}

%% --- status legend (SELF-DEMONSTRATING) ---
%% Each label word is rendered in its OWN marker color -- "verified" green+wavy,
%% "unverified" red+wavy, "exception" in its own color -- so the legend
%% exemplifies the markers rather than showing an abstract swatch (operator:
%% 自己説明的 / self-explanatory). No badge glyphs: every one of the 4 states is
%% a colored wavy underline.
%% NB: state names follow clew's unified schema 1.5 (the red state is "failed";
%% the internal color macro stays clewUnverified for back-compat with existing
%% clew_rendered.tex files).
\newcommand{\clewLabel}[2]{{\color{#1}\uwave{#2}}}
\newcommand{\clewLegend}{%
  \par\vspace{0.5em}\noindent{\footnotesize\color{gray}%
    \textbf{Clew status:}\ %
    \clewLabel{clewVerified}{verified}\quad
    \clewLabel{clewSuspect}{suspect}\quad
    \clewLabel{clewUnverified}{failed}\quad
    \clewLabel{clewException}{exception}\par}}

%% --- clew explainer section (clew provides \clewExplainerBody content;
%% writer provides this minimal, accessible layout). Author-placed: call
%% \clewExplainer where wanted; gated by the opt-in toggle so the call is a
%% no-op when off. Kept dependency-light (no tcolorbox) so the demo compiles. ---
\newcommand{\clewExplainer}{%
  \ifclewpresexplainer
    \par\medskip\noindent
    \fcolorbox{gray}{gray!6!white}{%
      \begin{minipage}{\dimexpr\linewidth-2\fboxsep-2\fboxrule\relax}
        \small
        {\bfseries How to read provenance marks}\par\smallskip
        \clewExplainerBody\par\smallskip
        \clewLegend
      \end{minipage}}\par\medskip
  \fi}

%% --- clew provenance INTRO section (replaces the old bottom legend) ---
%% A top-of-document boxed callout that EXPLAINS the four provenance statuses
%% with a worked, self-demonstrating example of each -- reusing \clewLabel so
%% each status word is shown in its own marker color/wavy underline, plus the
%% \clewExplainerBody prose. This is the explainer content PROMOTED to a
%% document-start section: the compile pipeline auto-injects \clewIntro right
%% after \end{frontmatter} (else after \begin{document}) when the `intro`
%% toggle is on, so a reader meets the legend at the TOP of the paper instead
%% of a cryptic key buried at the bottom. Gated by the toggle so an explicit
%% call is a no-op when off. Dependency-light (no tcolorbox) -- same layout
%% family as \clewExplainer so the demo compiles unchanged.
\newcommand{\clewIntro}{%
  \ifclewpresintro
    \par\medskip\noindent
    \fcolorbox{gray}{gray!6!white}{%
      \begin{minipage}{\dimexpr\linewidth-2\fboxsep-2\fboxrule\relax}
        \small
        {\bfseries Provenance marks}\par\smallskip
        \clewExplainerBody\par\smallskip
        \begin{itemize}\setlength{\itemsep}{2pt}\setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
          \item \clewLabel{clewVerified}{verified} --
                the value matches the analysis output that produced it.
          \item \clewLabel{clewSuspect}{suspect} --
                the source drifted since it was last checked; re-verify.
          \item \clewLabel{clewUnverified}{failed} --
                the value does not match its recorded source.
          \item \clewLabel{clewException}{exception} --
                verification could not be run for this value.
        \end{itemize}
      \end{minipage}}\par\medskip
  \fi}

%% Auto-placement: badge (+ opt-in legend_first) at top; attestation at the end.
%% Manual macros (\clewVerifiedBadge, \clewExplainer, \clewLegend, \clewIntro,
%% \clewSignature, ...) stay available for explicit placement.
%% legend_first reuses \clewLegend UNCHANGED, auto-placed at page-1 top (before
%% the badge). Opt-in and NOT in the master set, so it never double-renders.
%%
%% NOTE (redesign A/B): the Writer signature is NO LONGER auto-placed here.
%% \clewSignature stays defined for back-compat, but the "Compiled by SciTeX
%% Writer vX" mark now renders as a PER-PAGE bottom-right footer, injected by
%% the compile pipeline (signature_footer.tex) when the `signature` toggle (or
%% the standalone SCITEX_WRITER_SIGNATURE_FOOTER opt-in) is on -- decoupled from
%% this bottom colophon. Likewise the bottom \clewLegend is REPLACED by the
%% top-of-document \clewIntro section (auto-injected by the pipeline); the
%% macro stays defined for back-compat but is not auto-placed.
\AtBeginDocument{%
  \ifclewpreslegendfirst\clewLegend\fi
  \ifclewpresbadge\par\noindent\clewVerifiedBadge\par\fi}
\AtEndDocument{%
  \ifclewpresattest\clewAttestation\fi}

%% Safe fallbacks: if clew_rendered.tex was NOT input (no claims / layer off),
%% \clewval / \clewmark still compile (plain passthrough), and the aggregate
%% macros exist so the badge code never errors.
\providecommand{\clew@total}{0}
\providecommand{\clew@verified}{0}
\providecommand{\clew@allverified}{0}
\providecommand{\clew@version}{}
%% Writer version fallback (empty): the compile pipeline \def's this to the LIVE
%% scitex-writer version before \begin{document}; empty here so a standalone
%% compile still renders "Compiled by SciTeX Writer" without a version.
\providecommand{\writer@version}{}

\makeatother
%% EOF
